overview: Dredge is a combo strategy that mills its own library to populate the graveyard, then returns creatures to the battlefield to overwhelm opponents. It relies on card advantage engines and targeted disruption to protect its game plan. The deck operates independently of mana acceleration, focusing instead on graveyard recursion and board presence.
win_conditions: Overwhelming the opponent with a swarm of creatures from the graveyard
Executing a graveyard-based combo sequence to reduce opponent life
Leveraging mill effects to accelerate recursion and close the game

keep_criteria: Hands containing early mill or graveyard recursion
Hands with disruption to protect the game plan
Hands with card advantage engines to find missing pieces
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking interaction or graveyard synergy
Hands with only lands and non-interactive spells
Hands missing mill or recursion components

early_game: Play mill spells to rapidly fill the graveyard
Cast disruption to protect early recursion attempts
Use card advantage engines to draw into combo pieces
mid_game: Recycle creatures from the graveyard to apply pressure
Hold targeted counterspells for opponent's threats
Maintain board presence while continuing to mill
late_game: Flood the battlefield with recycled creatures
Use card advantage to outlast or find answers to hate cards
Close out games with overwhelming board state

key_cards.name: Bazaar of Baghdad
key_cards.notes: Use to draw into combo pieces or mill spells, and leverage its draw ability to find missing recursion components.

key_cards.name: Force of Negation
key_cards.notes: Hold for opponent's threats or to protect your graveyard recursion, ensuring your game plan isn't interrupted.

key_cards.name: Grief
key_cards.notes: Cast to rapidly fill the graveyard and enable dredge recursion, prioritizing it early to establish board presence.

sequencing_tips: Prioritize mill before recursion to ensure graveyard population
Hold disruption for key threats rather than using it preemptively
Use card advantage to find missing pieces before committing to recursion
Protect graveyard from hate by sequencing counterspells after opponent's interaction

matchups.advice: Out-grind with card advantage and protect your combo sequence
Mill quickly and use disruption on early threats to stabilize
Disrupt their sequence and mill to find answers before they win
